name: ws01: foundation — reconcile ADR-044 wasip1 vs .cargo/config wasip2 alias
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — "Deferred items / ADR-044 wasip2 migration" + Task List row 2.

Code citations:
- `docs/adr/adr-044-wasm-wasip1-target.md:31-37`
- `.cargo/config.toml` (`wasm` alias points at `wasm32-wasip2`, `release-wasm` profile)
- `rust-toolchain.toml` (channel 1.93)

## Problem / gap
ADR-044 says `wasip1` is the *current* primary target with migration to `wasip2` planned for Sprint 12, but `.cargo/config.toml`'s `wasm` alias already targets `wasm32-wasip2`. The two sources of truth disagree. Either Sprint 12 migration is partially done and the ADR is stale, or the alias change was premature and should be reverted. This must be settled before 0.7.0 cuts because anyone building WASM artifacts via the documented `cargo wasm` alias gets a different target than the ADR claims.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decide whether wasip2 is the new primary target.
- [ ] If yes: update ADR-044 (or supersede with a new ADR), confirm all WASM consumers (browser, wasi runner) build green on wasip2, update `docs/guides/` accordingly.
- [ ] If no: revert `.cargo/config.toml` alias to `wasm32-wasip1`.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh wasi` and `scripts/build.sh browser` both green on chosen target.
- [ ] Single source of truth documented for which WASI target ships.

## Dependencies
None blocking; should land before 0.7.0 publish to avoid downstream confusion.

## Notes
Rust 1.93 supports both targets. Cheap to align; cost is review + decision, not implementation.

name: ws01: bootstrap — split workspace from global at loader for PermissionResolver ceiling
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — TODO at `crates/clawft-core/src/bootstrap.rs:633` and Task List row 3. Same root cause as ws06 MW-2.

## Problem / gap
Current `bootstrap.rs` deep-merges the workspace overlay into a single `Config` upstream so workspace policy reaches `PermissionResolver` but `enforce_workspace_ceiling` never runs. Workspace permissions are not clamped against system-wide bounds. Acceptable for single-user kernels, blocks multi-tenant. Marked `TODO(v1.1)` in source.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Loader returns `(global: Config, workspace: Option<Config>)` or equivalent.
- [ ] `bootstrap` calls `PermissionResolver::new(global, Some(workspace))`.
- [ ] `enforce_workspace_ceiling` clamps workspace permissions per FIX-04 rules (level<=1, no budget/rate-limit increases, tools subset of global).
- [ ] Test: workspace `.clawft/config.json` granting elevated perms is silently clamped, with audit-log warning emitted.
- [ ] Multi-tenant scenario covered by integration test.

## Dependencies
Cross-listed with ws06 MW-2 (memory/workspace audit). Coordinate so the fix lands once.

## Notes
Single-tenant deploys are unaffected; promote to 0.7.x only if multi-tenant lands sooner.

name: ws01: rpc — implement Windows daemon transport (named pipes) for DaemonClient
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — TODO/stub at `crates/clawft-rpc/src/client.rs:54-80,62-64` and Task List row 4.

## Problem / gap
Non-Unix `DaemonClient::connect()` returns `None`. Comment says "Windows named-pipe transport is planned for v0.2"; we are at 0.6.19 — slipped 4+ minor versions. Windows users cannot run the daemon at all.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Implement named-pipe transport on Windows (or document explicit non-support).
- [ ] `DaemonClient::connect()` returns a working client on Windows.
- [ ] CI matrix includes Windows build of `clawft-rpc`.
- [ ] Update `docs/guides/` to reflect Windows status.

## Dependencies
Likely depends on ws01 version_check Windows port (separate item).

## Notes
Pure 0.7.0 publish does not require Windows support, but the slipped TODO is a poor signal. Could be deferred to 0.9.x if Windows demand stays low.

name: ws01: rpc — replace version_check curl shell-out with reqwest
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 5; code at `crates/clawft-rpc/src/version_check.rs:63-83`.

## Problem / gap
`version_check` shells out to `curl` for the GitHub release-check. This assumes `curl` exists on every host (it doesn't on Windows or minimal containers/Alpine), and shells from a foundation crate. `reqwest` is already a transitive workspace dep.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Replace `std::process::Command::new("curl")` with a `reqwest` GET request.
- [ ] 24h cache behavior preserved.
- [ ] Test covers offline / 404 / parse-failure paths.
- [ ] Crate compiles + works on Windows + minimal Linux container.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Small mechanical fix; gates the Windows daemon story.

name: ws01: platform — implement OPFS-backed BrowserFileSystem persistence
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 6; header note at `crates/clawft-platform/src/browser/fs.rs:1-9`, companion at `crates/clawft-platform/src/browser/mod.rs:7`. Cross-references ws01 BrowserEnvironment item.

## Problem / gap
`BrowserFileSystem` is currently in-memory `HashMap`-backed. PWA users lose all state on reload. Comment says "acceptable for the current stub/MVP phase".

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `BrowserFileSystem` reads/writes through OPFS (Origin Private File System) bindings.
- [ ] Falls back gracefully when OPFS unavailable.
- [ ] State survives page reload in a smoke test.
- [ ] Existing `clawft-wasm` tests pass.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/guides/browser.md` or equivalent.

## Dependencies
Upstream `web-sys` OPFS bindings stability (was unstable at port time).

## Notes
Pair with `BrowserEnvironment` persistence (separate item). Required for any real WeftOS PWA.

name: ws01: platform — land OPFS-or-equivalent BrowserEnvironment persistence
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 13; code at `crates/clawft-platform/src/browser/env.rs`.

## Problem / gap
`BrowserEnvironment` is in-memory only. Same UX consequence as the FS issue: env variables (e.g. API keys) are lost on reload.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `BrowserEnvironment` persists to OPFS or `IndexedDB`.
- [ ] Round-trip test confirms env survives reload.
- [ ] `BrowserPlatform::env()` retains its async signature.
- [ ] Config sensitive values continue to redact in Debug.

## Dependencies
Pairs with the `BrowserFileSystem` OPFS work; same upstream binding constraints.

## Notes
Low priority because secrets-in-browser is a security smell anyway; document the trade-off.

name: ws01: kernel-config — wire LogQuantizedStubConfig + SimdDistanceStubConfig runtime
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 7; types at `crates/clawft-types/src/config/kernel.rs:780,810` (foundation surface) waiting on ruvector-core PR #352 (kernel runtime).

## Problem / gap
Two `*StubConfig` types are serializable stubs in `clawft-types`; their runtime backends sit in `clawft-kernel` and require ruvector-core PR #352. Foundation surface ready; runtime missing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ruvector-core PR #352 merged & released (upstream tracking).
- [ ] `clawft-kernel` consumes the new ruvector-core APIs and removes the stub guard.
- [ ] Round-trip test: deserialize config -> instantiate runtime -> use.

## Dependencies
Blocked by ruvector-core PR #352 upstream.

## Notes
Not a 0.7.0 blocker — stubs deserialize fine; runtime path is gated.

name: ws01: security — rationalize lenient validate_mcp_tool_name vs strict variant
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 8; code at `crates/clawft-core/src/security/mod.rs:448-483`. Cross-listed with ws03 03-11 (MCP wildcard tool-name validation).

## Problem / gap
`validate_mcp_tool_name` (lenient) returns `Ok(())` for any input that looks "local-ish"; only the strict variant is called at registration. The lenient helper is a code-smell — either it's unused (delete) or it's used somewhere (document why both exist). Risk: future caller picks the wrong helper and creates a security gap.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Audit all call sites of both variants.
- [ ] Either delete the lenient helper or rename + add module docs explaining when each is appropriate.
- [ ] No production caller uses lenient unless intentional and justified in source comment.
- [ ] Unit test pins the chosen contract.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Release-gate because it touches the MCP attack surface; pair with ws03 03-11.

name: ws01: rpc — add chain.append RPC for weaver soul promote
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 9; `docs/handoff.md:60`; `crates/clawft-cli/src/commands/skills_cmd.rs` (publish path); `crates/clawft-weave/src/commands/soul_cmd.rs:246` (TODO marker `TODO(agent-core-v1.1)`). Cross-listed with ws06 MW-3.

## Problem / gap
`weaver soul promote` falls back to writing a local audit log because the daemon doesn't expose a public `chain.append` RPC. Witness records meant to land in the chain currently sit in `<workspace>/.weftos/audit/soul-promote.log`. Marked `TODO(agent-core-v1.1)`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add `chain.append` request/response types to `clawft-rpc`.
- [ ] Implement handler in `clawft-weave`/daemon that proxies to `ChainManager`.
- [ ] `weaver soul promote` switches to RPC, retains the local audit log as redundant durable record.
- [ ] Auth: only privileged callers can append (governance check).
- [ ] Integration test: promote → assert chain entry present.

## Dependencies
Cross-listed with ws06 MW-3; align so the work lands once.

## Notes
Not a 0.7.0 blocker — local audit log is functionally durable; promotes to 0.8.x.

name: ws01: foundation — run ADR-010 v0.3 cancel-correctness audit on mesh select! branches
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 10; ADR at `docs/adr/adr-010-keep-tokio.md:14-15`.

## Problem / gap
ADR-010 calls for a v0.3 audit of `select!` branches in mesh networking code (foundation runtime decision). We are at 0.6.19 with no audit completed. Mesh code lives in stream 04 (mesh) but the ADR governs the foundation runtime.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Catalog every `tokio::select!` site in mesh code.
- [ ] For each, verify branches are cancel-safe per the ADR's checklist.
- [ ] Document findings or fixes in an audit memo.
- [ ] Add lint or doc-comment convention preventing regression.

## Dependencies
None, but coordinate with mesh workstream.

## Notes
Low risk (no observed issue), but the ADR explicitly tracks this as a deliverable.

name: ws01: security — decide whether clawft-security is publish=true
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 11; `crates/clawft-security/Cargo.toml:3`.

## Problem / gap
`clawft-security` has `publish = false`, meaning downstream consumers cannot use the 50+ audit checks via crates.io. Reasonable for an internal helper, but the 50+ check engine is library-shaped. Decide before the 0.7.0 publish.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded in an ADR or in `Cargo.toml` comment.
- [ ] If publish=true: README + docs.rs metadata in place; license + version pinning consistent with workspace.
- [ ] If publish=false: rationale documented; no surprise to external integrators.

## Dependencies
Folds into the version-drift fix (WEFT-8) timing.

## Notes
Publishability decisions are sticky after the first crates.io upload, so make the call before 0.7.0.

name: ws01: types — decide deny_unknown_fields lint mode for Config
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 12; code at `crates/clawft-types/src/config/mod.rs:1-5`.

## Problem / gap
`Config` accepts both camelCase and snake_case via `#[serde(alias)]` and silently ignores unknown fields. Forward-compat is good; typo-resistance is bad. No off-by-default lint mode for typo detection.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add an opt-in `deny_unknown_fields` mode (env var or CLI flag).
- [ ] When enabled, loading a config with stray keys produces a clear error.
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged (silent ignore).
- [ ] Unit test covers both modes.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Small ergonomic win; no urgency.

name: ws01: platform — document config_loader Layer 2 sync vs Layer 3 async asymmetry
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 14; code at `crates/clawft-platform/src/config_loader.rs:30-65`. Cross-listed with ws06 MW-12 (3g-review residue).

## Problem / gap
`discover_config_path` uses sync `Path::exists()` for Layer 2 (home-dir JSON) but Layer 3 (workspace overlay) is async via `fs.exists().await`. Asymmetry is documented inline but not in any ADR or guide. Layer 2 cannot be mocked, hence `tests/overlay_probe.rs` is `#[ignore]`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ADR or `docs/guides/configuration.md` section explains the asymmetry and the rationale.
- [ ] If feasible, lift Layer 2 to async (would unblock the ignored overlay probe test).
- [ ] If not, document the testing gap and the workaround.

## Dependencies
Intersects ws06 MW-4 (overlay probe test).

## Notes
Docs-only alternative is acceptable for 0.7.0.

name: ws01: cli — remove TODO(E1) and TODO(C5) markers in workstream-I notes
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 15; `.planning/development_notes/03-critical-fixes-cleanup/workstream-I-type-safety/notes.md`.

## Problem / gap
`TODO(E1)` and `TODO(C5)` markers (Discord ResumePayload, interactive slash commands) live in workstream-I notes. Their sub-streams have shipped (or been re-scoped); the markers are stale.

## Acceptance criteria

None.

name: ws01: cli — replace skills_cmd derived-on-first-sign placeholder pubkey with real Ed25519
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 16; code at `crates/clawft-cli/src/commands/skills_cmd.rs:793-803`.

## Problem / gap
`weft skills keygen` writes the literal string `(derived on first sign)` as the public-key file when the `signing` feature is not compiled in. Real Ed25519 derivation only happens at publish-time. Users running keygen without `signing` get a non-functional placeholder file but no error.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either (a) compile signing in by default and always derive a real key, or (b) error clearly when `signing` feature is off ("keygen requires --features signing").
- [ ] Tests cover both build configurations.
- [ ] `docs/skills/` updated to describe the supported workflow.

## Dependencies
Intersects ws04 task #11 (skill signing trust root docs).

## Notes
Release-gate because anyone publishing a skill without `signing` ends up with a corrupt keypair file.

name: ws01: planning — close out improvements.md Phase-5 sprint-tracker
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — Task List row 17; `.planning/development_notes/02-improvements-overview/sprint-tracker.md`, `.planning/improvements.md`.

## Problem / gap
The Phase-5 sprint-tracker still shows MVP / Full-Vision checkboxes from Week 8/12. All foundation-relevant items (A1-A9, B1-B9, I1-I8, J1-J7) are checked but the doc itself was never closed; it gives the illusion of work-in-progress to a future reader.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Mark sprint-tracker as `closed` with a final-state summary.
- [ ] Move any still-open items into Plane (this audit captures most).
- [ ] Same for `.planning/improvements.md`.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Docs hygiene before 0.7.0 publish.

name: ws01: planning — archive stale 00-initial-sprint codebase-map / planning-summary
description_md:

`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — "Orphaned work" section; `.planning/development_notes/00-initial-sprint/{codebase-map,planning-summary}.md`, `.planning/development_notes/00-initial-sprint/phase3/{exit-criteria-review,phase3-status,round3-summary,round4-summary}.md`.

Snapshot of the original Python → Rust port; pinned to nanobot lineage; stale relative to the WeftOS rebrand. Useful history but no living tracking.

Docs hygiene only.

name: ws01: types — clean up panic! macros in test-only canvas/provider/agent_bus arms
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/01-core-platform.md` — "Open questions" item 8; `crates/clawft-types/src/canvas.rs:259,287,318`, `provider.rs:775`, `agent_bus.rs:255,270,287`.

## Problem / gap
`panic!` macros live inside `#[cfg(test)]` match arms. Harmless (cfg-gated) but stylistically loud. Prefer `unreachable!` with rationale or proper `assert!` macros.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Replace `panic!` calls with `unreachable!("…")` or domain assertion macros.
- [ ] Same test outcomes.
- [ ] Lint clean.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Pure cleanup; safely deferrable.

name: ws03: routing — apply tier check to fallback model selection
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-06; security review §2.3, Q3.

Code: `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/tiered_router.rs` (fallback resolution path).

## Problem / gap
The security review recommended subjecting the fallback model to the same tier check, but the current code does not appear to gate the fallback by `max_tier`. A misconfigured `routing.fallback_model: anthropic/claude-opus-4-5` would let zero_trust users hit elite tier — bypassing the entire permission system.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Locate fallback resolution branch.
- [ ] Apply `PermissionResolver`-driven tier check before returning the fallback.
- [ ] Tier-mismatch case routes to a deny + reason or to a tier-appropriate alternate, never the disallowed model.
- [ ] Test: zero_trust user hits failover; assert fallback respects max_tier.
- [ ] Document the precedence in `routing.md`.

## Dependencies
Sits next to 03-09 (reason-redaction) — same code path.

## Notes
High-priority security fix; release-gate.

name: ws03: routing — HMAC the cost-tracker persistence file
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-07; security review §2.4, Q4.

Code: `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/cost_tracker.rs` persistence path.

## Problem / gap
Persistence file uses Unix mode 0600 but no HMAC / checksum. A local user with file access can reset their spend by editing the JSON — defeating the budget enforcement. Security review recommended HMAC keyed on a server-side secret.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Generate or load a server-side HMAC key on first launch (stored separately, mode 0600).
- [ ] Sign the cost-tracker JSON on write; verify on load.
- [ ] Tampered file produces a clear error (and optionally rebuilds from chain audit log).
- [ ] Test covers tamper detection.
- [ ] Key rotation path documented.

## Dependencies
None urgent; mild interaction with chain-event audit log.

## Notes
Release-gate because cost enforcement is a tier-cap promise to operators.

name: ws03: routing — reject window_seconds=0 in Phase H validation
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-08; security review §2.5, Q5.

## Problem / gap
Rate-limiter `window_seconds = 0` is documented as "functionally unlimited" but Phase H validation does not reject it. A misconfigured rate limit silently disables rate limiting.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add validation rejecting `window_seconds == 0` (or any non-positive value).
- [ ] Test confirms error path.
- [ ] Existing legit configs unaffected.
- [ ] Documented in `routing.md`.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Trivial; release-gate because silent failure of rate limiting is a security regression vector.

name: ws03: routing — redact RoutingDecision.reason to avoid info disclosure
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-09; security review T-06, Q6.

## Problem / gap
`RoutingDecision.reason` includes verbatim user metadata (`complexity={:.2}, tier={}, level={}, user={}`). When the reason is surfaced through error messages or audit logs, raw user identifiers and policy details leak to caller scope.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Truncate or redact `reason` before user-facing surfaces.
- [ ] Keep full reason internally for debug logs (gated on log level).
- [ ] Unit test asserts redaction in user-facing branch.
- [ ] Document the contract ("reason is operator-debug, not user-safe").

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Release-gate; pair with 03-06.

name: ws03: routing — audit-log model_override bypasses (escalation already logs)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-10; security review §2.3 + "Orphaned work" `permissions.rs` 757 lines.

Code: `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/permissions.rs`.

## Problem / gap
Escalation events log at `warn!` per security review recommendation, but `model_override` bypasses do not appear to. This means an admin lifting tier filtering via `model_override: true` leaves no audit trail.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Emit `chain_event!` and `tracing::warn!` whenever a `model_override` overrides tier filtering.
- [ ] Test asserts the event fires with user_id + model + reason fields.
- [ ] Documented in `governance.md`.

## Dependencies
Intersects 03-19 (admin endpoint exposing routing-decision history).

## Notes
Release-gate because admin bypass without audit is a governance gap.

name: ws03: routing — add MCP tool-name namespace validation against wildcard ['*']
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-11; security review T-02 attack vector `exec__shell` via wildcard `['*']`. Cross-listed with ws01 lenient `validate_mcp_tool_name`.

Code: `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/permissions.rs` (tool-name validation; currently exact-match only).

## Problem / gap
Tool-name validation is exact-match only. A tool registered as `exec__shell` (double-underscore namespace) can be granted via wildcard `["*"]` allowlists, opening a shell-execution path even when the operator thinks they only allowed safe tools.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Validate the tool-name namespace pattern (no `__shell`, no `..`, etc.).
- [ ] Wildcard `["*"]` does not cover sensitive namespaces unless an explicit `"exec_shell"` (or similar) is in the allow list.
- [ ] Test: register `exec__shell`, set allowlist to `["*"]`, assert deny.
- [ ] Document the namespace policy.

## Dependencies
Pair with ws01 lenient validator cleanup.

## Notes
High-priority security; release-gate.

name: ws03: routing — scaffold fuzz targets for 8 attack surfaces
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-12; `.planning/sparc/phase4/01-tiered-router/security-review.md` — 8 fuzz-target proposals.

Targets:
- `routing_config_parsing`
- `permission_resolution`
- `cost_tracker_concurrent`
- `rate_limiter_unique_ids`
- `auth_context_threading`
- `tool_permission_glob`
- `escalation_chain`
- `budget_persistence`

## Problem / gap
No `fuzz/` directory exists in any crate. Security review identified 8 fuzz targets covering parsing, concurrency, glob matching, and persistence; none scaffolded.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crates/clawft-core/fuzz/` (or workspace-level) created with `cargo-fuzz` skeleton.
- [ ] All 8 targets compile and run for at least 60s without findings.
- [ ] CI nightly job runs fuzz for a bounded time.
- [ ] README documents how to extend.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Deferrable to 0.8.x; not strictly a 0.7.0 gate but listed as medium-severity by the security reviewer.

name: ws03: routing — resolve CONS-002 (DashMap vs RwLock<HashMap> benchmark)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-13; `.planning/development_notes/01-tiered-router/consensus-log.md:443`.

## Problem / gap
CostTracker and RateLimiter implemented as `RwLock<HashMap>` (no new dep). Performance trade-off vs DashMap was never benchmarked under contention. Entry remains OPEN pending real production data.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Build a contention benchmark (criterion) for both data structures.
- [ ] Decide based on numbers; document in consensus-log.
- [ ] If switching, ship the change in a single PR.
- [ ] Otherwise, close CONS-002 with rationale.

## Dependencies
Production telemetry from 0.7.x deployments would inform.

## Notes
Research-y; defer.

name: ws03: routing — resolve CONS-003 final review (escalation security)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-14; consensus-log.md:443.

## Problem / gap
Escalation security model mitigated by FIX-04 + FIX-06; final review marked "at Gate C+G" but no completion stamp logged. Either the review happened and was never marked, or it never happened.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Re-walk the FIX-04 / FIX-06 mitigations against current code.
- [ ] Sign off in consensus-log with date + reviewer.
- [ ] Any new gaps spawn Plane items.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Mostly process; release-gate because escalation is the highest-blast-radius routing path.

name: ws03: routing — resolve CONS-006 (config validation boundary)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-15; consensus-log.md:443.

## Problem / gap
Validation lives in `routing_validation.rs` but the boundary between deserialization-time rejection (serde) and post-load validation was never finalized. Some checks are duplicated; some are only in one place.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Catalog every validation check.
- [ ] Decide for each: serde-time (`#[serde(deserialize_with)]`) vs post-load.

- [ ] Tests pin both paths.

None.

name: ws03: pipeline — wire D1 per-path advisory locks for parallel tool execution
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-16; `.planning/development_notes/05-pipeline-reliability/d-perf/notes.md:14-16`.

## Problem / gap
D1 parallel-tool implementation explicitly notes "Per-path advisory locks not implemented yet (future hardening)". The SPARC plan listed advisory locks as the D1 mitigation for race-condition risk (likelihood=Medium, impact=High, score=6). Current implementation runs tool calls in parallel via `futures::join_all` with no filesystem-level guard against two tools writing the same path concurrently.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Implement per-path advisory lock (`fcntl(F_SETLK)` on Unix, equivalent on Windows or a process-local lock map).
- [ ] Lock acquired before any FS-mutating tool; released on completion.
- [ ] Test: two parallel writes to same path serialize.
- [ ] No regression in tool-call throughput for non-overlapping paths.

## Dependencies
None urgent; intersects ws04 sandbox path canonicalization.

## Notes
Release-gate because D1 was a reliability-sprint deliverable left half-done.

name: ws03: pipeline — wire evolution_ready flag → mutation.rs GA loop (ADR-017 flywheel)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/03-pipeline-routing.md` — Task 03-17; `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/mutation.rs` (413 lines, complete GA implementation), `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/learner.rs` (TrajectoryLearner sets `evolution_ready: true`).

## Problem / gap
`mutation.rs` is a fully-wired genetic-algorithm implementation (selection, crossover, mutation operators, population state) but **not invoked** from any production code path. `TrajectoryLearner` sets `evolution_ready: true` after threshold; no caller reads the flag and triggers mutation. ADR-017's "self-improvement flywheel" exists as code, not as a running feature.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Define a production trigger surface (background tick? RPC?) that consumes `evolution_ready` and runs `mutation::evolve()`.
- [ ] Persist the evolved population (probably via substrate or kernel).
- [ ] End-to-end test: synthetic trajectories trigger evolution; new prompt variant reaches the agent loop.
- [ ] ADR-017 updated with the chosen wiring.

## Dependencies
ADR-017 amendment; possibly substrate persistence.

## Notes
Deferrable to 0.8.x; flagship feature but not 0.7.0-blocker.

`RetryModel` trains from observed retry outcomes but the `Default` impl resets to untrained. Daemon restarts lose all learned retry curves. Persistence path unclear.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Choose persistence target (substrate topic, JSON file under `~/.clawft/state/`, or kernel hnsw).

- [ ] On clean shutdown, persist weights.
- [ ] Test: train, restart, assert continuity.

The null-content deserializer (`8b05d868`) covers the Nemotron tool-call case. OpenAI vision API and others emit content as an array of content blocks (`[{type: "text", text: "..."}, {type: "image_url", ...}]`). name: ws04: architecture — decide fate of 8 orphaned clawft-plugin-* crates
description_md:
## Source

## Problem / gap
Eight workspace-member native plugin crates (`clawft-plugin-{browser, calendar, cargo, ci, containers, git, npm, oauth2}`) ship to crates.io at `0.6.6`, each with full Tool-trait impls, but no consumer in the workspace. Only `clawft-plugin-treesitter` is wired (via `clawft-kernel/Cargo.toml:65`'s `treesitter` feature). Every workspace `cargo check` rebuilds 8 unused crates.

Decision options:
- external consumers exist (downstream WeftOS / external integrators) → keep decoupled
- planned for a `weft full` extras bundle → wire it up
- legacy from pre-ADR-036 architecture → move out of workspace / archive

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One of: ADR + feature-gate-bundle (e.g. `weft --features full-plugins`), separate-repo migration, or documented downstream-consumer manifest.
- [ ] If feature-gated: kernel or CLI wires them under a single feature.
- [ ] If separate-repo: workspace member entries removed; repo URLs documented in `docs/plugins/`.
- [ ] Workspace `cargo check` no longer rebuilds the orphans (or only when feature on).
- [ ] crates.io publish strategy aligned with version-drift fix (WEFT-8).

## Dependencies
Intersects WEFT-8 (workspace deps version drift).

## Notes
P0 release-gate decision; the answer affects publish + CI.

name: ws04: architecture — ADR documenting clawft-core vs clawft-kernel ToolRegistry split + migration
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/04-plugin-skills.md` — Task 4; deferred item 6.

Code: `crates/clawft-core/src/tools/registry.rs:339` (used by agent loop), `crates/clawft-kernel/src/wasm_runner/registry.rs:18` (ADR-036, hierarchical, sync `BuiltinTool` trait).

## Problem / gap
Two `ToolRegistry` implementations with different Tool traits (async `dyn Tool` vs sync `dyn BuiltinTool`), different signature requirements, and overlapping responsibilities (both gate-check, both filter). ADR-036 says "All future tool registration ... must follow this pattern: register in the overlay, not the base" but the agent loop today still talks to the `clawft-core` registry, not the kernel one. **No ADR records the merger / migration plan.**

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New ADR (or ADR-036 amendment) explicitly states: which registry owns what, when migration happens, what the end-state looks like.
- [ ] Decision on whether to merge the two trait shapes or keep them.
- [ ] If migrating: timeline + breakage matrix.
- [ ] If keeping both: clear contract that prevents drift.

## Dependencies
ADR-036 amendment.

## Notes
P0; release-gate because dual-tracked registries is an architecture decision a 0.7.0 publish locks in.

Code: `crates/clawft-plugin/src/manifest.rs:11` (JSON/YAML PluginManifest); `crates/clawft-cli/src/commands/plugins_cmd.rs:289,401` (TOML `.weftos-plugin.toml` scaffold); `:365` (validator only checks TOML).

## Problem / gap
Two plugin manifest formats coexist:
- `clawft.plugin.json` — read by the WASM host's manifest reader.
- `.weftos-plugin.toml` — what `weft plugins create` scaffolds, what `weft plugins validate` checks.

A scaffolded plugin will pass `weft plugins validate` but **won't load via the WASM host**. This is a hard correctness bug.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Pick one canonical format (recommend `clawft.plugin.json` since it's already the runtime path).
- [ ] Update the scaffolder to emit the canonical format.
- [ ] Update the validator to check the canonical format + the WASM host loader path.
- [ ] Migration tool or doc for any existing `.weftos-plugin.toml` files.
- [ ] End-to-end test: scaffold → validate → load via WASM host.

## Dependencies
Intersects ws04 task 13 (manifest roundtrip test).

## Notes
P1 release-gate; current state lies to plugin authors.

name: ws04: skills — add per-skill allowed-tools intersection validator at load time
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/04-plugin-skills.md` — Task 7; deferred item 4; consensus.md SEC-SKILL-04.

## Problem / gap
Skill `allowed-tools` is intersected with the agent's `tool_access` only via the registry's `check_tool_permission` at execution time. The skill doesn't declare a precedence relationship to `allowed_tools` on the per-agent sandbox — so a project-trusted skill can request a tool that the agent's `SandboxPolicy.allowed_tools` would have allowed, and the skill metadata adds nothing. There is no "skill-declared tools must be a subset of agent-allowed tools at parse time" validator.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Skill loader validates `allowed_tools ⊆ agent.tool_access` at load time.
- [ ] Mismatch produces a clear error referencing the skill + the disallowed tool.
- [ ] Test: skill requesting a denied tool is rejected before any agent turn runs.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/skills/`.

## Dependencies
None urgent.

name: ws06: memory — route MemoryStore + SkillsLoader through WorkspaceContext
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — MW-1; WS-O3 / WS-Q11 / WS-Q12.

Code:
- `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/memory.rs:52-79` (`MemoryStore::new` resolves `~/.clawft/workspace/memory/` legacy)
- `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/skills.rs:186-211` (`SkillsLoader::new` resolves `~/.clawft/workspace/skills/`)

## Problem / gap
`MemoryStore::new` and `SkillsLoader::new` were written before workspace-aware bootstrap and never migrated. In a workspace-loaded daemon there are now two memory roots — the workspace-scoped one created by `WorkspaceManager::create` and the home-scoped one used by `MemoryStore::new`. Result: writes to MEMORY.md from a workspace-loaded daemon land in the home dir, not the workspace's `.clawft/memory/`. Users discover this when they `git status` the workspace and see no changes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `MemoryStore` and `SkillsLoader` constructions go through the loaded `WorkspaceContext`.
- [ ] When a workspace is active, `<workspace>/.clawft/{memory,skills}` is the resolved path.
- [ ] Fallback to home dir only when no workspace.
- [ ] Regression test: load workspace, write MEMORY.md, assert workspace file changed (not home dir).
- [ ] Same regression for SkillsLoader.
- [ ] Migration guide for existing single-user installs.

## Dependencies
Intersects ws01 bootstrap loader split (multi-tenant) but standalone fixable.

## Notes
P1 release-gate; the workspace-loaded daemon's memory writes silently land in the wrong place.

name: ws06: bootstrap — split workspace from global at loader for PermissionResolver ceiling (MW-2)
description_md:
## Source

## Problem / gap
Loader currently deep-merges workspace overlay into a single Config. `enforce_workspace_ceiling` is bypassed. Multi-tenant deployments cannot apply the security ceiling. `Fine for single-user kernels; needed for multi-tenant.`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Loader returns `(global, Option<workspace>)`.
- [ ] `bootstrap` calls `PermissionResolver::new(global, Some(workspace))`.
- [ ] `enforce_workspace_ceiling` clamps workspace permissions per FIX-04.
- [ ] Multi-tenant integration test.

## Dependencies

name: ws06: memory — rebuild memory.rvf.json when MEMORY.md changes (MW-6)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — MW-6; WS-O6.

## Problem / gap
`MemoryStore::search` is purely substring + case-insensitive. `memory_bootstrap` builds a vector index in parallel, but the index is built once at bootstrap. `memory_bootstrap` is idempotent and skips if the index file exists, which means **edits to MEMORY.md are not reflected in vector search until the index is manually deleted.**

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either:
  - (a) Check mtime in `bootstrap_memory_index` and re-index when `MEMORY.md` is newer than the index.
  - (b) Add a `weft memory reindex` command (and document the manual workflow).
- [ ] Reindex test: write to MEMORY.md, search via vector backend, find new content.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/memory.md`.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
P1 release-gate because vector-search staleness is silent and confusing.

name: ws06: substrate — emit chain_event! for session.append on every appended turn (MW-7)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — MW-7; WS-O5.

## Problem / gap
`chain_event!` for `session.create` fires only when neither cache nor disk has the session. There is no `session.update` event for appended turns, only on the file-not-existing branch of `append_turn`. A long-running session looks like one create event followed by a destroy. The hot loop activity is invisible to the chain.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Emit `session.append` on every appended turn.
- [ ] Sample-rate cap if volume is too high (configurable).
- [ ] Test: 100 turns produce 100 events (or sampled count).
- [ ] Documented in `governance.md`.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Deferrable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `--keep-data` opts out.

"Hard refusal is a v1.1 follow-up." Binding-thread mismatch currently downgrades to an annotation + warn log, never refuses. Reviewer-flagged as a security posture decision.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision: refuse / annotate / config-toggle.
- [ ] Implementation matches.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/security/identity.md`.
- [ ] Test pins the chosen behavior.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Deferrable; security posture call.

name: ws06: embeddings — pick fate of rvf_stub.rs vs rvf_io.rs (MW-15)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — MW-15; WS-O2.

## Problem / gap
`crates/clawft-core/src/embeddings/rvf_stub.rs` and `rvf_io.rs` both ship. The plan says rvf_stub gets *replaced* by rvf_io; if both are alive, callers need to know which to use.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either remove `rvf_stub.rs` (and update callers), or keep with a module-doc: "brute-force fallback for the no-rvf-feature build".
- [ ] Build matrix covers both feature configurations.
- [ ] No orphan code.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Release-gate because dual implementations rot fast.

name: ws06: workspace — document or drop per-agent tool_state/ subdirectory (MW-16)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — MW-16; WS-O7.

## Problem / gap
Per-agent workspace `tool_state/` subdirectory is created but no consumer in `clawft-core` writes there. Plugins are presumably the consumers (Contract 3.1).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Confirm whether any plugin (in or out of tree) writes to `tool_state/`.
- [ ] If yes: document in `docs/plugins/` and verify sandbox grants the path.
- [ ] If no: drop the subdirectory from `AGENT_WORKSPACE_SUBDIRS` (with migration note).

## Dependencies
Intersects ws04 plugin sandbox audit.

## Notes
Release-gate because empty subdirectories that look meaningful are confusing.

name: ws06: identity — route IdentityLoader::current through Platform::fs() (MW-17)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — MW-17; WS-O9.

## Problem / gap
`IdentityLoader::current` uses `std::fs::read_to_string` directly instead of going through `Platform::fs()`. The only sync, platform-bypassing read in the agent identity path. Cannot be exercised in WASM the same way the rest of the agent loop is.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `IdentityLoader::current` refactored to async, uses `Platform::fs().read_string()`.
- [ ] Or: explicit ADR-style comment that documents the deviation.
- [ ] If async: callers updated.
- [ ] WASM test exercises the path.

## Dependencies
May touch many call sites; assess blast radius first.

## Notes
Deferrable.

name: ws06: identity — define journal substrate read-on-every-turn path (WS-D1)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — WS-D1; `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/identity.rs:32-38`.

## Problem / gap
"No SOUL.journal write path — F1 seeds the empty journal file and stamps the soul_journal derived-write grant; F2's weaver soul promote reads it, diffs, and applies on confirmation. The journal is not consulted on every-turn loads." Any consumer that needs a per-turn read will need to design that path (likely via the same substrate, not a file watcher).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Identify whether any feature needs per-turn journal reads.
- [ ] If yes: design the substrate-backed read path.
- [ ] If no: document the deferral.

## Dependencies
None.

## Notes
Research-y; long horizon.

name: ws06: identity — substrate-backed Identity::source variant set (WS-D4 / WS-D5)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/06-memory-workspace.md` — WS-D4 + WS-D5; `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/identity.rs:79-89`.

## Problem / gap
`Identity::source` is `&'static str` placeholder; trait is forward-compatible. Error variants need to be added when substrate path lands (today only signals "files missing").

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] When substrate-backed identity path lands: introduce the new variant.
- [ ] Error variants distinguish IO from deserialization.
- [ ] Callers updated.

## Dependencies
Substrate-backed identity (post-F2 roadmap).

## Notes
Long horizon.
